mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production growth signals expanding goods-sector output and operating leverage that lifts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly changes in INDPRO show non-positive rank correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC <= 0.03) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals accelerating labor market deterioration that precedes consumer spending pullbacks and earnings downgrades, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE increased from 4.4% in Mar 2007 to 4.7% in Oct 2007
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE has risen >0.3pp in the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and increases in unemployment (UNRATE) indicate a self-reinforcing contraction in aggregate demand that corporate revenues cannot escape, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS and UNRATE vintage values show diverging direction in late 2007
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and UNRATE does not exhibit negative predictive power (Rank IC > -0.03) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, an acceleration in CPIAUCSL inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy for longer than equity markets discount, raising real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.76% in Oct 2007 with CPIAUCSL at 208.903
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, positive 20-day SPX returns occur in > 55% of months despite accelerating CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nominal retail sales (RSAFS) captures contemporaneous consumer spending strength that directly flows into top-line revenue surprises, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS monthly changes show non-positive rank correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC <= 0) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal a sustained contraction in goods-producing sector output that precedes broad-based earnings estimate reductions, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of observations following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and housing starts (HOUST) below 1,300,000 units tightens financial conditions while simultaneously depressing construction activity and related employment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.76% and HOUST at 1191 in Sep 2007
failure_condition: Joint behavior of FEDFUNDS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC > -0.03) during walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: During inflationary periods, simultaneous acceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) validates a wage-price spiral narrative that forces additional monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138421 and CPIAUCSL at 208.903 in Oct 2007
failure_condition: The interaction of PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL fails to negatively predict 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC > -0.03) over a 3-year rolling window.
